L1_STEERING_4c714c.name: STEERING
L1_STEERING_4c714c.original_description: The planning, measuring, monitoring and governance activities that shape and direct the European Parliament's Core and Supporting activities. The 'Steering' activities ensure that the Core and Supporting activities meet operational, financial, regulatory and legal requirements. The 'Steering' activities do not directly deliver value to the citizens of the European Union, but are necessary in order to ensure the EP operates effectively and efficiently and is accountable to all its stakeholders.
L1_STEERING_4c714c.refined_description: The STEERING capability represents the foundational governance and management framework that provides strategic direction, oversight, and control mechanisms across all European Parliament operations. This top-level capability category encompasses the essential planning, measuring, monitoring, and governance activities that ensure organizational effectiveness, regulatory compliance, and stakeholder accountability. While STEERING activities do not directly deliver citizen-facing value, they enable and optimize the performance of both Core and Supporting business capabilities through systematic oversight and strategic alignment. The capability integrates three critical sub-domains: Planning (strategic and operational planning processes), Monitoring & Reporting (performance measurement and transparency mechanisms), and Governance (institutional control and compliance frameworks). STEERING serves as the organizational nervous system that maintains institutional integrity, ensures resource optimization, and enables informed decision-making across all parliamentary functions.
L1_STEERING_4c714c.capability_keywords: governance, strategic planning, oversight, monitoring, compliance, accountability, performance management, institutional control, regulatory oversight, strategic direction, organizational effectiveness, stakeholder management, risk management, decision-making, transparency

L2_55d9653ce231.name: Planning
L2_55d9653ce231.original_description: The Planning area is targeting at creating a vision, a mission, and strategic objectives, and culminate in creating strategic and operational plans to ensure that the organization is moving in the desired direction.
L2_55d9653ce231.refined_description: The Planning capability encompasses the systematic development and articulation of organizational direction through vision creation, mission definition, and strategic objective formulation for the European Parliament. This foundational business area transforms high-level governance directives from the parent STEERING capability into actionable strategic and operational frameworks that guide institutional decision-making and resource allocation. The capability orchestrates the creation of comprehensive planning documents that cascade from strategic intent to operational execution, ensuring organizational alignment and coordinated movement toward desired outcomes. Planning serves as the critical bridge between governance oversight and operational delivery, enabling the Parliament to translate its democratic mandate into structured, measurable initiatives across its three specialized sub-capabilities: Strategic Planning, Operational Planning, and Budgetary Planning.
L2_55d9653ce231.capability_keywords: strategic planning, operational planning, vision development, mission articulation, objective setting, strategic objectives, organizational direction, planning frameworks, resource allocation, institutional planning, governance alignment, strategic initiatives, operational execution, planning processes, organizational strategy

L3_5ab781cfa5b4.name: Strategic Planning
L3_5ab781cfa5b4.original_description: Ability to develop and execute strategic and business plans that align IT direction with organisational objectives. Strategic planning covers defining an IT vision and principles, developing strategic plans, identifying and evaluating strategic options, managing resourcing, aligning with business priorities, engaging stakeholders through communication, managing related planning processes, and tracking and evaluating strategic outcomes. Business planning encompasses annual work programme development, defining programme objectives, managing planning assumptions, establishing roles and responsibilities, alignment planning with institutional priorities, communicating the annual work programme, reviewing and controlling programme execution, and managing ongoing operational commitments.
L3_5ab781cfa5b4.refined_description: Strategic Planning is a foundational sub-business area capability that enables organizations to establish long-term direction and translate high-level organizational vision into actionable strategic and operational frameworks. This capability encompasses the systematic development of IT vision, principles, and strategic plans that align technology initiatives with broader institutional objectives, while simultaneously managing annual work programmes that define specific deliverables, resource allocation, and performance metrics. As a core component of the broader Planning business area, Strategic Planning works in coordination with Operational Planning and Budgetary Planning capabilities to ensure comprehensive organizational direction-setting. The capability enables stakeholder engagement, strategic option evaluation, resource optimization, and continuous monitoring of strategic outcomes to maintain alignment between IT investments and institutional priorities. Through its dual focus on strategic vision-setting and tactical programme management, this capability serves as the critical bridge between organizational aspirations and executable business plans.
L3_5ab781cfa5b4.capability_keywords: strategic planning, business planning, IT strategy, organizational alignment, vision development, strategic objectives, work programme, resource allocation, stakeholder engagement, performance monitoring, strategic evaluation, business priorities, programme management, institutional planning, strategic outcomes

L3_c5219ed98765.name: Operational Planning
L3_c5219ed98765.original_description: Once a common understanding on purpose, vision, goals has been agreed upon, emphasis has to be put on the specific business objectives for each DG to best serve the EP Administration's common strategies. This is the time to come to the definition objectives and of an adequate portfolio of projects which will enable the DG to provide new products and new processes while stopping out-dated functions. The DG's strategic programmes and projects to be incorporated into the Parliamentary Project Portfolio are adequately defined, with precise scope, timing, resources, demands for cooperation, accountability.
L3_c5219ed98765.refined_description: Operational Planning is a sub-business area capability that translates strategic direction into actionable business objectives and project portfolios for individual Directorates-General (DGs) within the European Parliament Administration. This capability enables DGs to define specific objectives, scope project initiatives, and establish resource requirements that align with the organization's common strategic framework established through Strategic Planning. It encompasses the systematic development of project portfolios, including the identification of new products and processes while discontinuing outdated functions, ensuring each DG's operational activities directly support the Parliament's overarching mission. The capability ensures precise definition of project scope, timing, resource allocation, cooperation requirements, and accountability structures for integration into the Parliamentary Project Portfolio, bridging the gap between high-level strategic intent and operational execution.
L3_c5219ed98765.capability_keywords: operational planning, business objectives, project portfolio management, resource allocation, project scoping, program definition, operational execution, portfolio integration, project timing, accountability structures, cooperation planning, process optimization, function modernization, DG alignment, strategic implementation

L3_61d9b63012c9.name: Budgetary Planning
L3_61d9b63012c9.original_description: Ability to plan and manage the organisational budget through structured processes that ensure alignment with business priorities. This encompasses defining the budget scope to capture all relevant expenditure categories, establishing and executing budget processes including timelines, responsibilities and approval workflows, and ensuring business alignment so that budgetary allocations reflect strategic objectives and operational needs.
L3_61d9b63012c9.refined_description: Budgetary Planning is a core sub-business area capability that enables systematic financial resource allocation and management across organizational units through structured planning processes. This capability supports the parent Planning capability by translating strategic objectives and operational requirements into detailed financial frameworks, ensuring fiscal discipline and resource optimization. It encompasses the definition of comprehensive budget scope covering all expenditure categories, establishment of formal budget development processes with clear timelines and approval workflows, and alignment mechanisms that ensure budgetary allocations directly support strategic priorities and operational needs. As a foundational financial planning capability, it operates in coordination with Strategic Planning and Operational Planning sibling capabilities to ensure integrated organizational direction and resource deployment.
L3_61d9b63012c9.capability_keywords: budget planning, financial planning, resource allocation, expenditure management, budget processes, fiscal planning, budget alignment, financial governance, budget development, cost planning, budget approval, financial resource management

L2_6a57b5439343.name: Monitoring & Reporting
L2_6a57b5439343.original_description: This area enables the organization to monitor and report on the achievement of its objectives, both at strategic and operational level. The monitoring of corporate activities is based on the use of indicators meant to: ensure accountability in the use of public funds; identify the level of attainment of operational objectives; identify the level of attainment of strategic goals; identify areas of improvement; highlight the principles and direction of effort.
L2_6a57b5439343.refined_description: The Monitoring & Reporting capability enables systematic oversight and performance assessment across all European Parliament operations, serving as a critical component of the broader STEERING framework. This capability establishes the organizational capacity to track, measure, and communicate progress against both strategic objectives and operational targets through comprehensive indicator-based monitoring systems. It ensures accountability and transparency in the utilization of public resources while providing stakeholders with timely, accurate information about institutional performance and goal attainment. The capability encompasses two specialized sub-areas: Operational Reporting for day-to-day activity tracking and Budgetary Reporting for financial oversight, working in concert with Planning and Governance capabilities to form a complete management control system. Through systematic identification of performance gaps and improvement opportunities, this capability enables evidence-based decision-making and continuous organizational enhancement.
L2_6a57b5439343.capability_keywords: monitoring, reporting, performance measurement, indicators, accountability, oversight, strategic objectives, operational targets, progress tracking, performance assessment, transparency, public funds management, goal attainment, continuous improvement, evidence-based decisions

L3_39c5de5d040c.name: Operational Reporting
L3_39c5de5d040c.original_description: Activities in this sub-area ensure fulfillment of Article 66 (9) of the Financial Regulation: the authorising officer by delegation shall report to his or her institution on the performance of his or her duties in the form of an annual activity report containing financial and management information, including the results of controls.
L3_39c5de5d040c.refined_description: The Operational Reporting capability encompasses the systematic collection, analysis, and communication of performance data and management information to fulfill regulatory compliance requirements and support informed decision-making within the European Parliament. This capability enables authorizing officers by delegation to discharge their accountability obligations under Article 66(9) of the Financial Regulation through the production of comprehensive annual activity reports that integrate financial performance metrics, operational outcomes, and control effectiveness assessments. As a specialized component within the broader Monitoring & Reporting business area, this capability works in conjunction with Budgetary Reporting to provide holistic organizational oversight. The capability supports institutional transparency and governance by ensuring that performance data, control results, and management insights are systematically documented and communicated to relevant stakeholders and oversight bodies.
L3_39c5de5d040c.capability_keywords: operational reporting, annual activity reports, performance monitoring, financial reporting, management information, control results, accountability reporting, regulatory compliance, Article 66 Financial Regulation, authorizing officer duties, institutional reporting, performance assessment, governance reporting, transparency obligations

L3_1ee76b1d2202.name: Budgetary Reporting
L3_1ee76b1d2202.original_description: Ability to oversee budget performance and produce comprehensive financial reports that support informed decision-making across the organisation. This includes defining appropriate scope, granularity and sophistication of financial reporting, establishing metrics and reporting frameworks, ensuring data consistency across all budget reporting outputs, analysing and communicating future IT expenditure forecasts, managing and reporting on unit costs, maintaining effective reporting tools and processes, and raising awareness and communicating budgetary performance to relevant stakeholders.
L3_1ee76b1d2202.refined_description: The Budgetary Reporting capability enables the European Parliament to systematically monitor, analyze, and communicate financial performance across all organizational units through comprehensive budget oversight and reporting mechanisms. This capability encompasses the establishment of standardized financial reporting frameworks, metrics definition, and data consistency protocols that ensure accurate and timely budget performance visibility to support strategic decision-making processes. As a critical component of the broader Monitoring & Reporting business area, it specifically focuses on financial dimensions of organizational performance, complementing operational reporting capabilities to provide holistic oversight. The capability includes sophisticated forecasting functions for IT expenditure planning, unit cost management, and stakeholder communication processes that translate complex financial data into actionable insights for parliamentary leadership and administrative units.
L3_1ee76b1d2202.capability_keywords: budget monitoring, financial reporting, budget performance, expenditure forecasting, cost management, financial metrics, budget analysis, stakeholder communication, reporting frameworks, data consistency, unit costs, financial oversight, budget tracking, fiscal reporting, financial transparency

L2_888661ad3733.name: Governance
L2_888661ad3733.original_description: The Governance area has the purpose of ensuring the internal capability of setting up and maintaining a coherent and consistent organizational governance across all levels, better control through the management of risks and compliance with the given set of rules.
L2_888661ad3733.refined_description: The Governance business area establishes and maintains the foundational framework for organizational oversight, accountability, and control across all levels of the European Parliament. This capability enables the institution to implement coherent governance structures, ensure consistent decision-making processes, and maintain organizational integrity through systematic risk management and regulatory compliance. As a core component of the STEERING capability, Governance provides the essential control mechanisms that support strategic direction and operational excellence. The capability encompasses three critical domains: Strategic & Operational Governance for establishing governance frameworks, Risk Management for identifying and mitigating organizational threats, and Compliance Management for ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements and internal policies. This comprehensive governance approach enables the Parliament to maintain public trust, operational effectiveness, and institutional accountability while supporting informed decision-making across all organizational functions.
L2_888661ad3733.capability_keywords: governance, oversight, accountability, control, compliance, risk management, regulatory adherence, organizational framework, decision-making, internal controls, audit, policy enforcement, institutional integrity, governance structures, strategic oversight

L3_dbdda3fb5a7a.name: Strategic & Operational Governance
L3_dbdda3fb5a7a.original_description: Ability to establish and maintain effective IT governance structures that ensure alignment between IT activities and organisational objectives. This encompasses defining the governance context and guiding principles, establishing appropriate decision bodies and structures with clearly allocated decision rights, defining roles and accountabilities, and implementing the core governance cycle of evaluating options and performance, directing activities and investments, and monitoring outcomes to ensure continuous alignment with institutional priorities.
L3_dbdda3fb5a7a.refined_description: Strategic & Operational Governance represents the organization's ability to establish, implement, and continuously optimize IT governance frameworks that ensure strategic alignment between technology investments and institutional objectives. This capability enables the European Parliament to define governance contexts and guiding principles, establish decision-making bodies with clearly delineated authority and accountability structures, and execute comprehensive governance cycles that evaluate performance, direct strategic activities, and monitor outcomes. Operating within the broader Governance business area alongside Risk Management and Compliance Management capabilities, it provides the foundational oversight mechanisms that ensure IT initiatives support parliamentary functions and democratic processes. The capability encompasses both strategic governance (long-term direction setting and policy formulation) and operational governance (day-to-day decision-making and performance monitoring) to maintain continuous organizational alignment and effectiveness.
L3_dbdda3fb5a7a.capability_keywords: IT governance, strategic alignment, decision rights, governance frameworks, performance monitoring, institutional priorities, decision bodies, accountability structures, governance cycles, policy formulation, oversight mechanisms, strategic direction, operational control, organizational alignment, investment governance

L3_24c005e80067.name: Risk Management
L3_24c005e80067.original_description: Ability to manage enterprise-level risks through a structured framework encompassing the establishment and maintenance of an enterprise risk management framework that defines methodologies, roles and responsibilities, the maintenance of a comprehensive enterprise risk register that identifies, assesses and tracks organisational risks, and regular enterprise risk reporting to ensure decision-makers have visibility of the risk landscape and can take timely and informed action.
L3_24c005e80067.refined_description: The Risk Management capability enables the European Parliament to systematically identify, assess, monitor, and mitigate enterprise-level risks that could impact organizational objectives and operations. This capability establishes and maintains a comprehensive risk management framework that defines standardized methodologies, clear roles and responsibilities, and governance processes for enterprise-wide risk oversight. It encompasses the development and maintenance of a centralized risk register that captures, evaluates, and tracks organizational risks across all business areas, while providing regular risk reporting and analytics to support informed decision-making by leadership and governance bodies. As a critical component within the broader Governance business area, this capability works in conjunction with Strategic & Operational Governance and Compliance Management to ensure robust organizational oversight and control. The capability enables proactive risk identification, evidence-based risk assessment, and timely implementation of risk mitigation strategies to protect the Parliament's mission-critical functions and strategic objectives.
L3_24c005e80067.capability_keywords: risk assessment, risk identification, risk mitigation, enterprise risk management, risk register, risk monitoring, risk reporting, risk framework, risk governance, threat analysis, vulnerability assessment, risk controls, business continuity, organizational resilience

L3_375ecb007e7f.name: Compliance Management
L3_375ecb007e7f.original_description: Ability to ensure organisational compliance through assurance, internal control and personal data protection activities. This includes providing assurance legal advisory services, performing quality control and risk mitigation to maintain the integrity of operations. In the domain of personal data protection, it encompasses managing data subject rights, overseeing information life cycles, monitoring, reporting and enforcement of data protection regulations, fostering a data protection culture, defining roles, responsibilities and accountabilities, developing skills and competences, managing personal data acquisition, purpose limitation, adequacy, accuracy, retention and destruction, overseeing risk, security and access rights management related to personal data, establishing strategies, policies and controls, and managing data protection requirements in supplier relationships.
L3_375ecb007e7f.refined_description: Compliance Management is a critical sub-business area capability within the Governance framework that ensures the European Parliament maintains adherence to legal, regulatory, and internal standards through systematic assurance and control mechanisms. This capability encompasses comprehensive legal advisory services, quality control processes, and risk mitigation activities that safeguard operational integrity and organizational reputation. At its core, it establishes robust personal data protection governance including GDPR compliance, data subject rights management, information lifecycle oversight, and comprehensive monitoring and reporting frameworks. The capability enables the organization to proactively identify compliance gaps, implement corrective measures, and maintain a culture of regulatory adherence while managing supplier relationships and third-party compliance requirements. As a foundational element of the broader Governance business area, it works in conjunction with Strategic & Operational Governance and Risk Management capabilities to ensure comprehensive organizational oversight and accountability.
L3_375ecb007e7f.capability_keywords: compliance, assurance, internal control, data protection, GDPR, legal advisory, quality control, risk mitigation, regulatory adherence, data subject rights, information lifecycle, monitoring, reporting, enforcement, data protection culture, supplier compliance, personal data management, security controls, audit, governance

L1_CORE_c5d5df.name: CORE
L1_CORE_c5d5df.original_description: The activities that are core and fundamental to the purpose and role of the European Parliament (EP) in direct support of the goals and values of the European Union, such as promoting peace, freedom and prosperity. The European Parliament's principle areas of work include deciding on the EU laws (jointly with the Council of the EU) that affect the daily lives of the European Union's citizens; exercising budgetary powers jointly with the Council for the allocation of spending based on the EU's political priorities; the monitoring of the EU funds to ensure they are being used correctly. Activities classified as 'Core' should be able to clearly demonstrate a direct and intended outcome that has value for the European Union's citizens.
L1_CORE_c5d5df.refined_description: The CORE capability category encompasses the fundamental constitutional and democratic functions that define the European Parliament's essential role within the European Union's institutional framework. This top-level capability enables the Parliament to fulfill its primary mandate of representing EU citizens through three critical business areas: exercising legislative powers jointly with the Council to create binding EU law, wielding budgetary authority to allocate and oversee EU financial resources, and conducting non-legislative activities including democratic oversight and political engagement. The capability directly supports the Parliament's constitutional mission to promote European values of peace, freedom, and prosperity while ensuring democratic accountability and citizen representation. As the foundational capability category, CORE encompasses all parliamentary activities that deliver direct, measurable value to European Union citizens through democratic governance, legal framework development, and fiscal stewardship.
L1_CORE_c5d5df.capability_keywords: legislative powers, budgetary authority, democratic oversight, citizen representation, EU law creation, financial allocation, parliamentary governance, political engagement, constitutional mandate, democratic accountability, European values, fiscal stewardship, legal framework, institutional role, citizen value

L2_3616e967f7a3.name: Legislative, Budgetary and Non-Legislative Powers
L2_3616e967f7a3.original_description: Core Business Area containing fundamental procedures of European Parliamentary work that refer to its legislative, budgetary, and non-legislative related activities. Main legislative and non-legislative procedures for law-making are included here together with the oversight/control function for ensuring proper implementation of approved laws.
L2_3616e967f7a3.refined_description: The Legislative, Budgetary and Non-Legislative Powers business area encompasses the European Parliament's core constitutional functions that enable democratic governance and legislative oversight within the EU institutional framework. This capability enables the organization to execute its primary mandate through structured procedures for examining, adopting, and controlling EU legislation, managing budgetary processes, and conducting non-legislative activities including international agreement oversight and inter-institutional relations. As a foundational component of the CORE capability category, it orchestrates the Parliament's essential law-making and control functions through five specialized sub-capabilities covering legislation examination, budget adoption, international agreements, national parliament relations, and non-legislative procedures. This business area ensures the proper implementation and oversight of approved laws while maintaining democratic accountability and institutional balance within the European Union's governance structure.
L2_3616e967f7a3.capability_keywords: legislative procedures, budgetary control, law-making, parliamentary oversight, EU legislation, budget adoption, international agreements, democratic governance, institutional relations, legislative examination, parliamentary control, non-legislative procedures, legal oversight, constitutional functions, inter-institutional coordination

L3_8d06a89541fb.name: EU Legislation examination, adoption and control
L3_8d06a89541fb.original_description: This sub-business area describes the law making and law oversight powers of the EP exercised through different procedures: the Ordinary legislative procedure became the most popular way of implementation of legislative acts. This sub-business area also includes the supervisory and control powers that the EP has to ensure the correct implementation of EU law.
L3_8d06a89541fb.refined_description: This capability encompasses the European Parliament's core legislative and oversight functions within the EU's democratic governance framework. It enables the Parliament to examine, debate, amend, and adopt EU legislation through established procedures, with the Ordinary Legislative Procedure serving as the primary mechanism for co-decision making with the Council. The capability also empowers the Parliament to exercise supervisory control over the implementation and enforcement of EU law, ensuring compliance and accountability across EU institutions and member states. As a fundamental component of the Legislative, Budgetary and Non-Legislative Powers business area, this capability operationalizes the Parliament's constitutional role as co-legislator and guardian of democratic oversight in the European Union's institutional architecture.
L3_8d06a89541fb.capability_keywords: legislation examination, legislative adoption, ordinary legislative procedure, co-decision, law making, parliamentary oversight, supervisory control, EU law implementation, legislative scrutiny, compliance monitoring, democratic governance, institutional accountability, legislative procedures, law enforcement oversight

L3_ad8bf2b0a365.name: EU Budget adoption and control
L3_ad8bf2b0a365.original_description: Jointly with the Council the agreement and adoption of a multiannual financial framework of the Union’s revenue and expenditure covering a 7 year period. The examination and agreement between the EP and Council of the annual draft budget of the Union to be adopted for the forthcoming year, as well as the scrutiny of spending from the previous year.
L3_ad8bf2b0a365.refined_description: The EU Budget adoption and control capability encompasses the European Parliament's constitutional authority to jointly determine, approve, and oversee the European Union's financial framework and annual budgetary allocations. This capability enables the Parliament to collaborate with the Council in establishing multiannual financial frameworks spanning seven-year periods, which define the Union's revenue streams and expenditure priorities. Additionally, it empowers the Parliament to examine, negotiate, and adopt annual draft budgets for upcoming fiscal years while conducting rigorous scrutiny and control of previous year expenditures. As a core component of the Legislative, Budgetary and Non-Legislative Powers business area, this capability ensures democratic oversight of EU financial resources and maintains fiscal accountability across Union institutions. The capability operates through structured inter-institutional procedures that balance parliamentary representation with Council authority in budgetary decision-making processes.
L3_ad8bf2b0a365.capability_keywords: budget adoption, financial framework, multiannual planning, expenditure control, revenue management, fiscal oversight, budgetary scrutiny, inter-institutional negotiation, financial accountability, spending authorization, budget examination, fiscal governance, parliamentary approval, Council collaboration, financial planning

L3_10fc990d2ceb.name: International agreements
L3_10fc990d2ceb.original_description: The EP’s approval is required for most international agreements concluded by the EU. International agreements are defined as agreements between either non-EU States or third-party organisations and the EU and cover a wide range of areas such as international trade agreements, association and partnership agreements, international agreements aimed at protecting the environment or aimed at countering terrorism.
L3_10fc990d2ceb.refined_description: The International Agreements capability enables the European Parliament to exercise its constitutional approval authority over binding international commitments entered into by the European Union with non-EU states and third-party organizations. This capability encompasses the parliamentary review, assessment, and formal consent processes for diverse international instruments including trade agreements, association and partnership frameworks, environmental protection treaties, and counter-terrorism accords. As a critical component of the Legislative, Budgetary and Non-Legislative Powers business area, this capability ensures democratic oversight and legitimacy of the EU's external relations commitments. The capability operates through structured parliamentary procedures that evaluate the legal, political, and policy implications of proposed international agreements before granting the required institutional consent for their conclusion.
L3_10fc990d2ceb.capability_keywords: international agreements, parliamentary approval, consent procedure, treaty ratification, external relations, trade agreements, association agreements, partnership agreements, environmental treaties, counter-terrorism accords, democratic oversight, institutional consent, international commitments, third-party organizations, non-EU states

L3_5874e5c7e9fa.refined_description: The Relations with National Parliaments capability enables the European Parliament to maintain structured institutional cooperation and legislative dialogue with member state parliaments, supporting the implementation of Treaty-based inter-parliamentary cooperation frameworks. L3_2196e3b6e660.original_description: This sub-business area concerns the activities and parliamentary duties of the Members and Committees that are not directly related to the legislative cycle and budgetary powers of the Parliament, includes the following: special procedures, delegated and implementing acts, but also EP's own initiative procedures.

L2_5a5acebc55a4.original_description: The provision and management of specialized and/or specific internal EP services and resources that enable the EP to deliver effectively its principle areas of work e.g. EU legislation adoption, conclusion of international agreements. These activities provide front line capabilities that are integral to the operation and resulting outcomes of the EP's other Core business areas, namely 'Legislative and Budgetary Powers' and 'Political Engagement and Relationship Management'.

L3_495428d80e4d.original_description: The sub-business area 'Elections and Establishment of Parliamentary Term' covers the activities relating to the elections of Members to the European Parliament, which take place every 5 years, and the establishment of the Parliamentary Officers and Bodies and governing frameworks following these elections.
L3_495428d80e4d.refined_description: The "Elections and Establishment of Parliamentary Term" capability encompasses the foundational governance processes that establish the democratic legitimacy and operational framework of the European Parliament following quinquennial elections. L3_10b43dac8c00.name: Legal Services
L3_10b43dac8c00.original_description: This sub-area has the purpose of describing the activities related to the provision of official legal opinions and advice to parliamentary committees throughout legislative procedures so that any legislation adopted by Parliament, any international agreement signed by the EU as well as the budget are fully in accordance with EU law, as well as describing the activities performed in relation to court proceedings on core-business topics.

L1_SUPPORTING_59bd41.name: SUPPORTING

While these capabilities do not directly deliver value to EU citizens, they are critical enablers that ensure the EP's core legislative, oversight, and representative functions can be performed efficiently and effectively. This includes ongoing budget monitoring to track expenditure against plans, variance management to identify and address deviations, asset management to track and maintain organisational assets throughout their lifecycle, and budget governance to ensure compliance with institutional financial regulations and internal control standards.
L3_a7ca46dc2d9d.refined_description: Budget Execution represents the operational core of financial management within the European Parliament's Financial & Accounting Services business area, enabling the systematic implementation of approved budgetary plans through comprehensive financial cycle management. This capability orchestrates the end-to-end execution of organizational spending, encompassing fund allocation and accounting processes, commitment management, invoice processing and payment execution, and recovery order issuance when necessary. The capability provides essential budget monitoring and variance management functions that ensure expenditure alignment with strategic plans while maintaining rigorous compliance with institutional financial regulations and internal control frameworks. Budget Execution works in coordination with sibling capabilities including Treasury & Accounting, Procurement, Grants, and Assets Management to deliver integrated financial stewardship across the organization's operational spectrum.
L3_a7ca46dc2d9d.capability_keywords: budget execution, financial cycle management, fund allocation, budget monitoring, expenditure tracking, payment processing, invoice management, budgetary commitments, variance analysis, financial compliance, recovery orders, budget governance, financial controls, spending authorization, fiscal management

L3_4fdee458e521.name: Treasury & Accounting
L3_4fdee458e521.original_description: The Treasury & Accounting sub-business area is comprised of the following processes: General accounting and Budgetary accounting (financial data, accounts receivable, accounts payable), Follow up of debts, Bank reconciliation, Preparation of period-end closing (Balance sheet, Results, Cash flow), Opening and closure of banking accounts: only the accounting officer can open and close the accounts of the EP.
L3_4fdee458e521.refined_description: The Treasury & Accounting capability encompasses the fundamental financial record-keeping and cash management functions that ensure accurate financial reporting and regulatory compliance within the European Parliament's Financial & Accounting Services business area. This capability enables the organization to maintain comprehensive financial control through general and budgetary accounting processes, including management of accounts receivable and payable, debt monitoring, and bank reconciliation activities. The capability supports critical period-end financial reporting by preparing balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements that provide transparency and accountability to stakeholders. As a core component of financial stewardship, this capability works in coordination with sibling capabilities such as Budget Execution and Procurement to ensure integrated financial management, while maintaining exclusive authority for banking account management through designated accounting officers.
L3_4fdee458e521.capability_keywords: treasury management, general accounting, budgetary accounting, accounts receivable, accounts payable, debt collection, bank reconciliation, financial reporting, balance sheet preparation, cash flow management, period-end closing, banking account administration, financial stewardship, accounting officer, financial compliance

L3_3a052708aa2c.name: Procurement
L3_3a052708aa2c.original_description: Ability to manage the end-to-end procurement lifecycle from strategic sourcing through supplier management to software and solution acquisition. In sourcing, this covers aligning procurement strategy with organisational objectives, defining procurement scope, making sourcing decisions, preparing and managing calls for tenders, selecting suppliers, providing procurement legal advisory services, and managing contract preparation, closing and service transition. Supplier management encompasses supplier integration and governance, selection, communication and innovation management, contract administration, framework contract execution, order and fulfilment management, performance management and contract compliance, continuity of supply assurance, and overall supplier engagement. For software and solution acquisition, it includes understanding organisational needs, selecting appropriate providers, and managing the transition of acquired solutions into the operational environment.
L3_3a052708aa2c.refined_description: The Procurement capability encompasses the organization's ability to strategically acquire goods, services, and solutions through a comprehensive end-to-end lifecycle management approach. This capability enables the European Parliament to align procurement activities with organizational objectives while ensuring efficient resource utilization, regulatory compliance, and value optimization across three core domains: strategic sourcing (including tender management and supplier selection), supplier relationship management (covering integration, performance monitoring, and contract administration), and software/solution acquisition (from needs assessment through operational transition). As a critical component of Financial & Accounting Services, Procurement works in conjunction with Budget Execution, Treasury & Accounting, and Assets Management capabilities to ensure comprehensive financial stewardship and resource governance. This capability directly supports organizational operational effectiveness by establishing robust supplier ecosystems, managing contractual relationships, and facilitating the acquisition of mission-critical resources and technologies.
L3_3a052708aa2c.capability_keywords: procurement, sourcing, supplier management, contract management, tender management, acquisition, vendor selection, supplier integration, contract administration, procurement strategy, supplier governance, framework contracts, order management, performance management, contract compliance, supply assurance, solution acquisition, procurement lifecycle, strategic sourcing

L3_b9a05b28041f.name: Grants
L3_b9a05b28041f.original_description: The Grants sub-area has the purpose of ensuring effective management of grants, that are direct financial contributions, by way of donation, from the budget in order to finance: either an action intended to help achieve an objective forming part of a European Union policy (action grants); or the functioning of a body which pursues an aim of general European interest or has an objective forming part of a European Union policy (operating grants).
L3_b9a05b28041f.refined_description: The Grants capability encompasses the comprehensive lifecycle management of European Union grant funding mechanisms, serving as a critical financial stewardship function within the broader Financial & Accounting Services business area. This capability enables the organization to strategically allocate and monitor direct financial contributions from the EU budget through two primary funding streams: action grants that support specific initiatives aligned with EU policy objectives, and operating grants that sustain organizations pursuing general European interests. The capability ensures rigorous oversight of grant disbursement, compliance monitoring, and performance evaluation to maximize the impact of EU financial resources. As a sub-capability of Financial & Accounting Services, it operates in coordination with Budget Execution, Treasury & Accounting, and Procurement capabilities to maintain integrated financial governance and accountability across the European Parliament's funding operations.
L3_b9a05b28041f.capability_keywords: grant management, financial contributions, EU funding, action grants, operating grants, budget allocation, grant disbursement, compliance monitoring, financial stewardship, donation management, policy funding, European interest funding, grant lifecycle, financial oversight, funding mechanisms

L3_d93c697221f2.name: Assets Management
L3_d93c697221f2.original_description: Asset management sub-area deals with the major asset classes of the EP: Tangible assets contain various subclasses, including current assets and fixed assets. Current assets include inventory, while fixed assets include such items as buildings and equipment. Intangible assets are nonphysical resources and rights that have a value to the firm because they give the firm some kind of advantage in the market place.
L3_d93c697221f2.refined_description: Assets Management is a critical sub-business area capability within the European Parliament's Financial & Accounting Services that provides comprehensive stewardship and lifecycle management of all organizational assets. This capability enables the systematic identification, valuation, tracking, and optimization of both tangible assets (including current assets such as inventory and fixed assets such as buildings, equipment, and infrastructure) and intangible assets (including intellectual property, licenses, and other non-physical resources that provide competitive advantage). The capability ensures proper asset classification, depreciation management, and strategic asset utilization while maintaining compliance with financial reporting standards and supporting informed decision-making across the organization. Assets Management works in coordination with sibling capabilities including Budget Execution for capital planning, Treasury & Accounting for financial reporting, and Procurement for asset acquisition processes.

L3_35270f1f296b.name: Information Security
L3_35270f1f296b.original_description: Information security has the following principal objectives: a. To guarantee that information, regardless of the medium that holds it, is always available for authorised needs and protected from unauthorised accesses and alterations; b. To safeguard European Parliament premises housing information from theft, tampering with, malicious wilful damage and all other unlawful forms of processing; c. In the event of a failure or a breach, to assess the damage caused, limit its consequences, identify the perpetrators and adopt the necessary remediation measures without delay, including appropriate judicial actions if deemed necessary.
L3_35270f1f296b.refined_description: The Information Security capability establishes and maintains comprehensive protection frameworks for the European Parliament's information assets across all media formats and storage systems. This capability ensures continuous availability of information for authorized users while implementing robust controls to prevent unauthorized access, modification, or disclosure of sensitive parliamentary data. As a specialized component within the broader Security business area, Information Security works in coordination with ICT Security and Physical Security capabilities to create layered defense mechanisms. The capability encompasses proactive risk management, incident response protocols, damage assessment procedures, and remediation strategies to maintain the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of critical parliamentary information assets. This foundational capability enables secure legislative operations, protects democratic processes, and ensures compliance with data protection requirements across all European Parliament activities.
L3_35270f1f296b.capability_keywords: information protection, data security, access control, authorization, confidentiality, integrity, availability, incident response, risk assessment, security breach management, information governance, data classification, security monitoring, threat detection, remediation

L3_1b37abf59083.refined_description: The Citizen and Stakeholder Communication capability encompasses the European Parliament's strategic external communication functions, enabling systematic engagement with EU citizens and broader stakeholder communities through multi-channel content strategies. As a core sub-capability within the broader Communication business area, it enables the Parliament to fulfill its democratic mandate by ensuring transparent, accessible, and engaging public discourse about EU legislative activities and institutional functions. The capability integrates crisis communication protocols and leverages the European Parliament Liaison Offices (EPLOs) network to maximize reach and local relevance, while maintaining consistent messaging aligned with institutional objectives.

L3_2b6b38f4cad0.name: Internal communication
L3_2b6b38f4cad0.original_description: Ability to coordinate, prepare, deliver and monitor communication activities directed at engaging and informing all staff across the European Parliament, promoting organisational achievements, fostering transparency and awareness over internal offerings. This encompasses managing end-user mass communications including service notifications, establishing and implementing a communication strategy aligned with the organisation's mission and the wider institutional communication approach, and developing a broad range of written and audio-visual content for internal channels such as newsletters and digital publications. It further includes promoting an innovation mindset through dedicated digital platforms where staff can share best practices, publish articles, run discussions and gather ideas, supported by coaching and training programmes including events, seminars and learning opportunities in the area of innovation. Additionally, it covers the active management of digital portals and corporate intranet content to ensure information is relevant, maintained, updated and targeted to selected audiences, as well as the facilitation of data science and innovation communities through dedicated platforms that encourage knowledge sharing, data literacy and the adoption of data-driven technologies across the institution.
L3_2b6b38f4cad0.refined_description: Internal Communication is a foundational sub-business area capability within the European Parliament's Communication business area that enables effective organizational cohesion through strategic information dissemination and engagement across all parliamentary staff. This capability encompasses the design, execution, and monitoring of comprehensive internal communication strategies that promote transparency, organizational awareness, and innovation adoption through multiple channels including digital platforms, newsletters, intranet portals, and specialized communities of practice. The capability facilitates knowledge sharing, data literacy advancement, and innovation mindset cultivation by providing dedicated platforms for staff collaboration, best practice sharing, and continuous learning opportunities. As a critical enabler of organizational effectiveness, this capability ensures that internal stakeholders remain informed about institutional developments, service offerings, and strategic initiatives while fostering a culture of innovation and data-driven decision making. This capability operates alongside sibling capabilities such as Media Relations and Multi-Channel Publishing to create a comprehensive communication ecosystem that supports both internal engagement and external stakeholder management.

L3_cfd8af30190c.name: Multi-Chanel Publishing
L3_cfd8af30190c.original_description: Ability to make information and content available across multiple formats — both digital and print — and diverse communication channels, encompassing the full publishing lifecycle from client order management through design, production and distribution. This includes managing client requests via a centralised service desk, providing graphic design services for visual content creation spanning print products, motion graphics and promotional materials, and performing content editing tasks such as language concordance, multilingual versioning, formatting, proofreading and page layout across the 24 official EU languages. It covers digital publishing of products such as interactive PDFs, infographics, animations, ePosters and eBanners, as well as print publishing including brochures, leaflets, posters, roll-up banners and institutional stationery. The capability extends to operating print shop facilities for both colour and black-and-white printing, managing digital and secure printing and scanning services, distributing printed matter to Members and staff, aggregating and structuring information from multiple source formats into semantic data, designing automated publication workflows and self-service templates, indexing documents using defined taxonomies, ensuring digital accessibility of all products, and establishing and maintaining printing standards and methods to streamline operations.
L3_cfd8af30190c.refined_description: Multi-Channel Publishing represents a comprehensive content production and distribution capability that enables the European Parliament to deliver institutional information across diverse communication channels and formats. This capability orchestrates the complete publishing lifecycle, from initial content requests through final distribution, supporting both digital and print media production in all 24 official EU languages. As a core component of the Communication business area, it provides essential content creation services including graphic design, multilingual editing, automated workflow management, and accessibility compliance to ensure consistent institutional messaging. The capability operates through integrated service delivery models encompassing centralized service desk operations, professional design services, production facilities, and distribution networks that serve both internal stakeholders and external audiences. By maintaining standardized publishing processes and self-service capabilities, this function ensures scalable, quality-controlled content delivery that supports the Parliament's broader communication and stakeholder engagement objectives.

L3_43998620f857.original_description: Ability to manage enterprise architecture, business process management and capability assessment to ensure alignment between organisational strategy and IT delivery. Enterprise architecture management includes establishing and governing an architecture framework, defining architecture processes, planning, aligning architecture with business goals, managing communication and stakeholder relationships, building appropriate organisational structures and skills, deploying architecture tools, and demonstrating architecture value. Business process management encompasses process architecture design, process automation, governance, improvement and stakeholder management, supported by defined standards and methods, appropriate technologies, and dedicated organisational support. Capability assessment management provides the frameworks and methods for planning and executing assessments, evaluating current capabilities against target states, and defining development roadmaps with clear scope of implementation.

L3_6736d870f95c.name: Data Management
L3_6736d870f95c.original_description: Ability to govern, manage and leverage organisational data assets across the full data lifecycle, encompassing data analytics, data management, enterprise information management and knowledge management. Data analytics includes corporate-level data production, integration and utilisation, data governance, establishing and maintaining data standards and methods, open data transformation, and fostering data analytics learning and knowledge sharing. Enterprise information management covers information strategy, governance, valuation, life cycle management, content management, metadata and master data management, data provisioning, standards and policies, controls, business continuity management, leadership, and the competences and tools needed to support information activities. Data management ensures consistent data governance and quality across the organisation. Knowledge management fosters a culture of knowledge discovery, capture and sharing, supported by skill development programmes and appropriate tools and technologies to make organisational knowledge accessible and reusable.
L3_6736d870f95c.refined_description: Data Management is a comprehensive sub-business area capability within the Information Technology domain that enables the European Parliament to systematically govern, manage, and leverage its organizational data assets throughout their complete lifecycle. The capability enables evidence-based decision-making, regulatory compliance, and operational efficiency by establishing robust data governance frameworks, standards, and policies while supporting the Parliament's digital transformation initiatives. As a foundational IT capability, it works synergistically with sibling capabilities such as Enterprise Architecture and IT Portfolio Management to ensure data-driven parliamentary operations and public transparency through open data initiatives.

L3_802f92f50081.name: IT Solution Delivery
L3_802f92f50081.original_description: Ability to deliver IT solutions through the complete development and application lifecycle, from understanding user needs through design, development, integration, testing, deployment, ongoing product management and end-of-life decommissioning. This includes managing the entire life of an application — from initial idea and assessment through acquisition, development and operation to retirement — as well as defining tasks performed at each step in the software development process. It covers IT solutions design, environment management, application configuration management, deployment and release management, toolchain management, team formation, and the collection of feedback and analytics to drive continuous improvement. Quality assurance is integral, covering functional testing, regression testing, system integration testing, system tests, unit integration testing, user acceptance tests, user-friendliness tests, smoke testing, security testing, black box test design, walkthroughs, reviews, software manuals quality assurance, and testing tools and methodology management. User experience design encompasses researching, informing, designing, evaluating and communicating user experiences to ensure solutions meet user needs. Digital accessibility management ensures inclusive design through accessibility awareness raising, guideline definition, testing, training, promotion and enforcement, advisory services and document remediation.
L3_802f92f50081.refined_description: IT Solution Delivery represents the comprehensive capability to design, develop, deploy, and maintain technology solutions throughout their complete lifecycle within the European Parliament's Information Technology business area. This capability encompasses the end-to-end process from initial user requirements analysis through solution architecture, development, testing, deployment, and eventual decommissioning, ensuring that all IT solutions align with organizational needs and quality standards. The capability integrates quality assurance practices, user experience design, and digital accessibility management to deliver inclusive, high-quality solutions that serve parliamentary functions effectively. It enables the organization to systematically manage application portfolios, implement robust development methodologies, and maintain continuous improvement through feedback collection and analytics. This capability works in conjunction with sibling capabilities such as IT Portfolio and Project management and Enterprise Architecture to ensure cohesive technology delivery across the institution.

L3_7c4676d165fd.name: IT Infrastructure Management
L3_7c4676d165fd.original_description: Ability to design, implement, operate and continuously improve IT infrastructure across all technology domains. This encompasses management of application platforms, connectivity and network infrastructure — including administering and managing computer networks together with their related security aspects, fault analysis, performance management, provisioning of networks and maintaining quality of service — as well as data centre environments including configuration management databases, change management, release management, physical layer services (space, cooling, power, grounding, cabling, power usage effectiveness), and telecommunications facilities and networks for transmitting and receiving information by electrical or electronic means. It further covers end-user IT facilities including hardware (computers, laptops, tablets, smartphones, printers), software (operating systems, office applications, web browsers and digital workplace enablement), printing services, storage systems, infrastructure-related software and software-defined infrastructure, and multi-functional device provisioning. IT service infrastructure management covers the full lifecycle including design of infrastructure solutions aligned with service requirements, implementation of new or modified infrastructure, ongoing operation to ensure availability and performance, and continuous improvement to optimise infrastructure efficiency, resilience and alignment with evolving organisational needs.
L3_7c4676d165fd.refined_description: IT Infrastructure Management represents the European Parliament's comprehensive capability to design, implement, operate, and continuously optimize the complete technology infrastructure ecosystem that underpins all digital operations and services. L3_2b2fcffa742c.name: IT Operations Management
L3_2b2fcffa742c.original_description: Ability to manage day-to-day IT operations ensuring stable, secure and efficient technology services across the organisation. This includes managing the full asset lifecycle from acquisition through deployment to disposal, capacity planning to ensure adequate resources, security management of operational environments, and performance monitoring and optimisation. It covers end-user equipment configuration management, identity management to control access to systems and resources, infrastructure availability, continuity and recovery management to minimise disruption, request, incident and problem management for responsive operational support, and management of server, mainframe and high-performance computing environments.
L3_2b2fcffa742c.refined_description: IT Operations Management represents the European Parliament's operational capability to maintain continuous, secure, and optimized technology service delivery across all organizational functions. L3_2c14dfe56f21.name: Service Management
L3_2c14dfe56f21.original_description: Ability to establish and maintain effective liaison and long-term relationships between the IT function and other organisational units to foster mutual understanding, align interests and minimise conflicts. This includes advocating for business needs by helping IT stakeholders understand where technology can best enable organisational value, as well as advocating for IT by promoting awareness of the IT function's current and potential contributions to external stakeholders. It encompasses maintaining awareness of developments within relationships to track and act on business intelligence, routing emerging risks, opportunities and exceptions to appropriate forums, and designing information collation principles and tools so the IT function can recognise relevant business changes. The capability also covers designing and executing communication programmes to keep stakeholders informed of IT developments and future plans, managing inter-institutional relationships with other EU institutions to share experience and align interests, and providing moderation, facilitation and mediation services to assist in conflict resolution and unblock situations involving multiple stakeholders. Relationship management is supported by defined methodologies for consistent practices, prioritisation frameworks to focus effort on the most significant relationships, and maintained relationship profiles that capture key information about stakeholders.
L3_2c14dfe56f21.refined_description: Service Management represents the European Parliament's strategic capability to orchestrate effective relationships between IT functions and organizational stakeholders, ensuring technology initiatives align with parliamentary business objectives. This capability enables the organization to bridge technical and business domains through systematic liaison management, stakeholder advocacy, and conflict resolution services that optimize IT value delivery. Operating within the broader Information Technology business area, it encompasses relationship governance, communication program execution, and inter-institutional coordination with other EU entities to leverage shared experiences and align technological interests. The capability employs structured methodologies, prioritization frameworks, and stakeholder profiling to maintain consistent relationship practices while proactively identifying business intelligence, emerging risks, and strategic opportunities that inform IT decision-making.
